title: T-003 — cost_centers (hierarchy) + gl_account_mappings schema (§6.1 rows 2/5, §11.1 V-FIN-SETUP-04/05)
prompt:
# T-003 — cost_centers hierarchy + gl_account_mappings schema

PRD: docs/prd/10-FINANCE-PRD.md §6.1 rows 2 and 5; §11.1 V-FIN-SETUP-04 (no parent_id cycle), V-FIN-SETUP-05 (mapping required per cost_category); §12.4 R15 adapter mapping; §14.1 L2 per-tenant variation.
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create Drizzle migration + ORM entries for `cost_centers` (self-referencing hierarchy via parent_id) and `gl_account_mappings` (cost_category → D365 GL account). Enforce cycle prevention via SQL function + BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE trigger (V-FIN-SETUP-04) and 1:1 uniqueness per (org_id, cost_category) for GL mappings.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0051_cost_centers_gl_mappings.sql`:
   - `CREATE TABLE cost_centers` (id, org_id, code TEXT, name TEXT, parent_id UUID NULL FK→cost_centers(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT, production_line_id UUID NULL, allocation_basis TEXT CHECK IN ('labor_hours','machine_hours','units'), d365_dimension_code TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, ext_jsonb JSONB DEFAULT '{}', created_at, updated_at; UNIQUE(org_id, code)).
   - `CREATE TABLE gl_account_mappings` (id, org_id, cost_category TEXT CHECK IN ('material','labor','overhead','waste','freight'), d365_account_code TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (d365_account_code ~ '^\d{4}-[A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]+-[A-Z]+$'), offset_account_code TEXT, d365_journal_name TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true, updated_by UUID FK→users(id), updated_at; UNIQUE(org_id, cost_category) WHERE is_active=true).
   - Cycle-prevention function `prevent_cost_center_cycle()` + BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE trigger on cost_centers: WITH RECURSIVE walks parent_id chain; RAISE EXCEPTION if NEW.id appears in ancestor chain (V-FIN-SETUP-04).
   - Both tables: ENABLE + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; policy `<table>_org_context` uses `app.current_org_id()`.
   - Index `idx_cost_centers_parent ON cost_centers(org_id, parent_id)`.
2. Drizzle ORM entries `packages/db/src/schema/cost-centers.ts`, `gl-account-mappings.ts` + register in index.ts.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0051_cost_centers_gl_mappings.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/cost-centers.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/gl-account-mappings.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/cost-centers-cycle.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/gl-mappings.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- Given migration 0051 ran, when \d cost_centers inspected, then parent_id FK ON DELETE RESTRICT exists and trigger prevent_cost_center_cycle is registered.
- Given cost_centers A(parent=NULL) and B(parent=A), when UPDATE sets A.parent_id=B.id, then it fails with raised exception (V-FIN-SETUP-04). AND given a deeper chain A→B→C→D, when UPDATE sets A.parent_id=D, then trigger also detects cycle and raises exception.
- Given gl_account_mappings with d365_account_code='INVALID', when INSERT runs, then it fails CHECK regex.
- Given two active gl_account_mappings for same (org_id, cost_category), when second INSERT runs, then it fails partial unique index. AND given pg_policies on both tables, when listed, then qual/withcheck reference app.current_org_id() (no GUC reads).
_(AC count consolidated from 6→4 by Fixer F13 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost))_

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: write two test files asserting cycle prevention (3 scenarios) + GL regex + partial unique on active mapping + RLS policy text inspection.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test cost-centers-cycle gl-mappings`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement cycle detection in application code only — the trigger is the SoT (UI could be bypassed via direct SQL).
- Do not use ON DELETE CASCADE on parent_id — cost center hierarchy survives parent deletion only via RESTRICT (operator must re-parent first).
- Do not loosen the d365_account_code regex — the format `\d{4}-[A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]+-[A-Z]+` is fixed by D365 chart of accounts per §12.4 + Apex `[ORG-CONFIG]`.
- Do not allow more than one active mapping per (org_id, cost_category) — V-FIN-SETUP-05 is hard.
- Do not skip RLS / FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.

pipeline_inputs.description: cost_centers self-ref hierarchy with cycle-prevention trigger + gl_account_mappings with 1-active-per-category constraint.
pipeline_inputs.details: cost_centers has self-FK parent_id ON DELETE RESTRICT; BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE trigger walks ancestor chain via WITH RECURSIVE and raises exception on cycle (V-FIN-SETUP-04). gl_account_mappings has cost_category enum + d365 account regex matching Apex chart of accounts format + partial unique index ensures at most one is_active=true row per (org_id, cost_category) (V-FIN-SETUP-05). Both tables use foundation app.current_org_id() RLS pattern. [AC count consolidated from 6→4 by Fixer F13 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost)]
out_of_scope: GL Mapping modal UI (T-006 / FIN-021 lives with cost center admin)
acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0051 ran, when \d cost_centers inspected, then parent_id FK ON DELETE RESTRICT exists and trigger prevent_cost_center_cycle is registered.

Given cost_centers A(parent=NULL) and B(parent=A), when UPDATE sets A.parent_id=B.id, then it fails with raised exception (V-FIN-SETUP-04). AND given a deeper chain A→B→C→D, when UPDATE sets A.parent_id=D, then trigger also detects cycle and raises exception.
Given gl_account_mappings with d365_account_code='INVALID', when INSERT runs, then it fails CHECK regex.

Given two active gl_account_mappings for same (org_id, cost_category), when second INSERT runs, then it fails partial unique index. AND given pg_policies on both tables, when listed, then qual/withcheck reference app.current_org_id() (no GUC reads).
test_strategy: RED: cost-centers-cycle.test.ts asserts 3 cycle scenarios (direct, transitive, deep) + RLS policy text. gl-mappings.test.ts asserts regex CHECK + partial unique + RLS.
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test cost-centers-cycle gl-mappings'.
risk_red_lines: Do not implement cycle detection only in application code — trigger is SoT.
Do not use ON DELETE CASCADE on parent_id (RESTRICT required).
Do not allow more than one active GL mapping per (org_id, cost_category).
Do not skip RLS / FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
